item.content:encoded:
Jesus House Aberdeen is a pentecostal church in Aberdeen UK, part of the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG) worldwide. We are a sanctuary open to everyone, offering Sunday service, Bible study, youth ministry, and community outreach programs

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
Website Privacy Policy
(UK GDPR Compliant)

Last updated: 24 May 2026

This Privacy Policy explains how Jesus House of Aberdeen ("we", "us", "our") collects, uses, and protects your personal data when you use our website https://jesushouseaberdeen.org. We are committed to protecting your privacy and complying with the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR) and the Data Protection Act 2018.

1. Who We Are

Organization Name: Jesus House of Aberdeen
Registered Address: 20 Holburn Street, Aberdeen, AB10 6BT
Email: admin@jesushouseaberdeen.org
Website: https://jesushouseaberdeen.org

We are the data controller for the purposes of UK data protection law.

2. What Personal Data We Collect

We may collect and process the following information:

  • Name
  • Email address
  • Telephone number (if provided)
  • Company name and job title (if provided)
  • Information submitted through contact or enquiry forms
  • Communication preferences
  • Website usage data (via cookies and analytics)

We do not collect sensitive personal data such as passport details, National Insurance numbers, health information, or financial information.

3. How We Collect Your Data

We collect personal data when you:

  • Complete forms on our website
  • Contact us by email or phone
  • Subscribe to our communications
  • Engage with our services
  • Browse our website (via cookies and analytics tools)

4. How We Use Your Data

We use your personal data to:

  • Respond to enquiries
  • Provide our services
  • Manage client relationships
  • Send relevant communications and updates
  • Improve our website and services
  • Meet legal and regulatory obligations

We only use your data for legitimate business purposes.

5. Legal Basis for Processing

Under UK GDPR, we process your data on the following lawful bases:

  • Consent – where you have given clear permission
  • Contract – where processing is necessary to deliver services
  • Legitimate Interests – to operate and grow our business
  • Legal Obligation – where required by law

6. Marketing Communications

We may send you information about our services where:

  • You have opted in, or
  • We have a legitimate business relationship

You may unsubscribe at any time by following the link in our emails or contacting us directly.

7. Sharing Your Data

We may share your data with trusted third parties, including:

  • IT and website hosting providers
  • CRM and email marketing platforms
  • Professional advisers (legal, accounting, compliance)

All third parties are required to process your data securely and in accordance with the law. We do not sell your personal data.

8. International Data Transfers

Some of our service providers may store data outside the UK. Where this happens, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place in line with UK GDPR requirements.

9. Data Security

We take appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data, including:

  • Secure servers
  • Access controls
  • Encrypted communications where appropriate
  • Regular system monitoring

However, no online system is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

10. Data Retention

We retain personal data only for as long as necessary for:

  • The purposes for which it was collected
  • Legal, regulatory, or accounting requirements

When data is no longer required, it is securely deleted.

11. Your Data Protection Rights

You have the right to:

  • Access your personal data
  • Request correction of inaccurate data
  • Request deletion of your data
  • Restrict processing
  • Object to processing
  • Request data portability
  • Withdraw consent at any time

To exercise these rights, please contact us using the details above.

12. Cookies

Our website uses cookies to improve functionality and analyse usage. For more information, please see our Cookie Policy.

13. Third-Party Links

Our website may contain links to external websites. We are not responsible for the privacy practices of those websites.

14. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date.

15. Complaints

If you are unhappy with how we handle your data, you have the right to complain to the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO):

Website: ico.org.uk
Telephone: 0303 123 1113

We encourage you to contact us first so we can try to resolve any concerns.

Contact Us

If you have questions about how we handle your data or this Privacy Policy, please contact:

Email: admin@jesushouseaberdeen.org
Phone: +447769991731
Address: 20 Holburn Street, Aberdeen, AB10 6BT

item.title: Homepage
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
Preparing Saints for Heaven.
Manifestation.

Changing Our World.

Join Us

VISIT US DURING SERVICE HOURS

SonRize Service at 9 am
Celebration Service at 11 am

BIBLE VERSE OF THE DAY

But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel. - 1 Timothy 5:8

WELCOME TO JESUS HOUSE ABERDEEN

At Jesus house Aberdeen City of God, you'll find a sanctuary open to everyone. Whether you're a member, a traveller, or someone seeking a welcoming space, our doors are always open. We are a pentecostal church in Aberdeen UK and part of the Redeemed Christian Church of God family.

Learn More About Us

Watch us live

Join us online via Youtube Live every Sunday.

Open in Youtube https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GVbQykuZZtE

Our Mission

We are passionate about the local church,and are on a mission to see God`s kingdom established across the earth.
See how you can help.

Read More

THEME OF THE YEAR

JOIN OUR COMMUNITY

We welcome everyone into our fold. Weu2019d love for you to be part of our church family in Aberdeen.
Speak to one of our ushers after the Sunday
Celebration Service.

Visit our Service Hours:
SonRize Service at 9 am
Celebration Service at 11 am

We organise sessions for new church members to introduce them to life in our faith community.

Join Us

OUR TRAININGS

Discovery (or Rediscovery) Class

Discovering your identity in Christ and finding your specific purpose and God given gifts.

Baptismal Class

For new believers or those looking to publicly declare their faith through water baptism by immersion.
Covers the tenets of the faith and the ‘New Birth’ experience.

Workers in Training (WIT)

8-week course equipping members for active service (choir, ushers, scripture teachers).

School of Discipleship (SOD)

10-month course (one Saturday monthly) producing well-grounded disciples who understand spiritual warfare.

Bible College

Advanced theological education.

Discovery (or Rediscovery) Class

Discovering your identity in Christ and finding your specific purpose and God given gifts.

Baptismal Class

For new believers or those looking to publicly declare their faith through water baptism by immersion.
Covers the tenets of the faith and the 'New Birth' experience.

Workers in Training (WIT)

8-week course equipping members for active service (choir, ushers, scripture teachers).

School of Discipleship (SOD)

10-month course (one Saturday monthly) producing well-grounded disciples who understand spiritual warfare.

Bible College

Advanced theological education.

FAQs

What is the difference between a Pentecostal church and the RCCG?

Jesus House Aberdeen is a parish of the RCCG, a global Pentecostal denomination founded in Nigeria in 1952. The RCCG operates under a unified doctrinal and leadership structure, whereas independent Pentecostal churches operate autonomously.

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

What are the key differences between worship at JHA and other churches?

JHA blends classic biblical and Pentecostal doctrine with contemporary community life, modern technology, and diverse events for all age groups, combining theological depth with practical, active engagement.

Jesus House Aberdeen is a parish of the RCCG, a global Pentecostal denomination founded in Nigeria in 1952. The RCCG operates under a unified doctrinal and leadership structure, whereas independent Pentecostal churches operate autonomously.

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

JHA blends classic biblical and Pentecostal doctrine with contemporary community life, modern technology, and diverse events for all age groups, combining theological depth with practical, active engagement.

View More

JOIN THE MISSION AT JHA

Worship With Us

item.title: Global Connection
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
THE REDEEMED CHRISTIAN CHURCH OF GOD

Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and today, and forever. -Hebrews 13:8-

Join Us

OUR MISSION

  • To make heaven and attain everlasting life.

  • To take as many people as possible with us through evangelism and winning souls for Christ.

  • To have a member of RCCG in every family of all nations.

  • To accomplish this, holiness will be our lifestyle.

  • We will plant churches within five minutes walking distance in every city and town of developing countries and within five minutes driving distance in every city and town of developed countries.

  • We will pursue these objectives until every Nation in the World is reached for Jesus Christ our Lord.

WHAT WE BELIEVE

  • God the Father – Creator of Heaven and Earth, who made man in His own image and likeness.
  • The Holy Trinity – The three persons of the Godhead; God the Father, God the Son, and God the Holy Spirit Three in One.
  • The Virgin Birth – Jesus was conceived by the Holy Spirit and born of the Virgin Mary.
  • The Death & Resurrection of Jesus Christ – Jesus was crucified and died for the sins of mankind on the cross at Calvary, was buried and rose from the dead on the third day. He ascended into Heaven and is forever seated at the right hand of God the Father.
  • The Holy Spirit – The Comforter and Teacher of all things who dwell within us, uniting us to Jesus Christ.
  • The Baptism of the Holy Spirit – This is freely available to all who believe in Jesus Christ and the Holy Spirit and is manifested by the gift of speaking in unknown tongues.
  • Salvation – A gift from God through grace and faith in Jesus Christ; that all who believe in Him may be saved by turning from sin to repentance, trusting in His death and resurrection and are born again by the Holy Spirit.
  • The Blood of Jesus Christ – It was shed for us on the cross at Calvary, cleanses us from all sin and grants us access into the presence of God.
  • The Second Coming of Jesus Christ – Jesus will come again to Earth in glory and the dead in Christ will rise and those who are alive in Christ will be translated into the presence of God for eternity.
  • The Holy Bible – The infallible and authoritative word of God given to direct all men and women to salvation.

FAQs

What is the difference between a Pentecostal church and the RCCG?

Jesus House Aberdeen is a parish of the RCCG, a global Pentecostal denomination founded in Nigeria in 1952. The RCCG operates under a unified doctrinal and leadership structure, whereas independent Pentecostal churches operate autonomously.

How does JHA compare to other RCCG branches in Scotland for youth programmes?

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

Which Aberdeen church offers the best blend of traditional teaching and modern activities?

JHA aligns programmes with RCCG global frameworks covering Bible study, mentorship, talent development, and leadership training. Local differences between RCCG branches reflect resources and community demographics, not theological approach.

Jesus House Aberdeen is a parish of the RCCG, a global Pentecostal denomination founded in Nigeria in 1952. The RCCG operates under a unified doctrinal and leadership structure, whereas independent Pentecostal churches operate autonomously.

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

JHA aligns programmes with RCCG global frameworks covering Bible study, mentorship, talent development, and leadership training. Local differences between RCCG branches reflect resources and community demographics, not theological approach.

View More

Join the Mission at JHA

Join Us

  • To make heaven and attain everlasting life.

  • To take as many people as possible with us through evangelism and winning souls for Christ.

  • To have a member of RCCG in every family of all nations.

  • To accomplish this, holiness will be our lifestyle.

  • We will plant churches within five minutes walking distance in every city and town of developing countries and within five minutes driving distance in every city and town of developed countries.

  • We will pursue these objectives until every Nation in the World is reached for Jesus Christ our Lord.

{ -webkit-text-stroke: 1px currentColor; paint-order: stroke fill; }","_animation":"fadeInUp"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"f2427ab","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"
  • God the Father – Creator of Heaven and Earth, who made man in His own image and likeness.
  • The Holy Trinity – The three persons of the Godhead; God the Father, God the Son, and God the Holy Spirit Three in One.
  • The Virgin Birth – Jesus was conceived by the Holy Spirit and born of the Virgin Mary.
  • The Death & Resurrection of Jesus Christ – Jesus was crucified and died for the sins of mankind on the cross at Calvary, was buried and rose from the dead on the third day. He ascended into Heaven and is forever seated at the right hand of God the Father.
  • The Holy Spirit – The Comforter and Teacher of all things who dwell within us, uniting us to Jesus Christ.
  • The Baptism of the Holy Spirit – This is freely available to all who believe in Jesus Christ and the Holy Spirit and is manifested by the gift of speaking in unknown tongues.
  • Salvation – A gift from God through grace and faith in Jesus Christ; that all who believe in Him may be saved by turning from sin to repentance, trusting in His death and resurrection and are born again by the Holy Spirit.
  • The Blood of Jesus Christ – It was shed for us on the cross at Calvary, cleanses us from all sin and grants us access into the presence of God.
  • The Second Coming of Jesus Christ – Jesus will come again to Earth in glory and the dead in Christ will rise and those who are alive in Christ will be translated into the presence of God for eternity.
  • The Holy Bible – The infallible and authoritative word of God given to direct all men and women to salvation.

item.title: Who We Are
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
ABOUT US

Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and today, and forever. -Hebrews 13:8-

Join Us

OUR HISTORY

Jesus House Aberdeen, also known as City of God Church Aberdeen, is an international evangelical church
and pentecostal church in Aberdeen UK.
We are part of the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG) worldwide, established in 1952 by Rev. Josiah O. Akindayomi. RCCG is a growing church with parishes in over 178 countries. Pa Josiah Akindayomi died in 1980 at the age of 71, handing leadership to Pastor Enoch Adeboye.

2003

Our Senior Pastors are Dr Mark Igiehon and Pastor Alero Igiehon. Dr Mark, an Oil and Gas Lawyer with a PhD in International Environmental Oil and Gas Law, was sent from RCCG Jesus House Port Harcourt in 2003 with a church planting mandate.

2004 - 2008

In 2004, Jesus House Aberdeen was inaugurated with six members. The church previously met at the Banagask community centre until 2008.

Today

Today, we are a 'mother church' for many sister parishes locally and globally, including connections in Canada. Our worship centre (City of God) is at 20 Holburn Street Aberdeen, AB10 6BT.

THE CHURCH

You matter to God. You matter to us.

We are a pentecostal church that believes in Jesus Christ, a church that loves God and people. Overwhelmed by the gift of salvation we have found in Jesus, we have a heart for authentic worship, are passionate about the local church, and are on a mission to see God's kingdom established.

Learn More About Us

SENIOR PASTORS

Dr. Mark Oseyomwanbo Igiehon

Lead Pastor, in charge of Province

Dr Mark Igiehon is the Senior Pastor of RCCG Jesus House Aberdeen (City of God), a pentecostal church in Aberdeen, Scotland, and part of the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG) worldwide. He is a minister of the gospel by training and vocation, an evangelist, and a missioner who has led missions to many cities and nations. Dr Mark is a church planter who has led the planting of tens of churches across multiple cities and nations. He is a coach to many across the globe, including pastors, and has written books on many subjects, including the WisdomStreet series on practical wisdom for everyday successful living. Beyond his pastoral calling, Dr Mark is a lawyer by training, a strategist, and an advisor to leading corporations and governments. He brings extensive global experience from his career with Shell International. He is a Fellow at the University of Aberdeen Centre for Energy & Resources Law & Policy, where he teaches postgraduates and supervises doctoral candidates. He is also co-founder and chair of SONG (Success of Nations & Governments Organisation & Institute), focused on training and equipping people to contribute to successful and impactful government and public life. He is affiliated with the Singapore-based MDN (Making Disciples of Nations) network. 

PASTOR ALERO IGIEHON

Wife of Pastor in Charge of Province and Marriage Counseling

Pastor Alero Igiehon is an ordained minister and senior pastor in the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG) at Jesus House Aberdeen. She is also a trained Environmental and Business Improvement Specialist with over 20 years of experience in the Oil and Gas Industry. She has led and served in various capacities in the church, teaching and mentoring on biblical principles. She has been actively engaged in Marriage Preparation and Counselling ministry for over 20 years. Her desire is to see homes and christ centered marriages enjoy fruitfulness, peace, and joy. She is wife to Dr Mark Igiehon, a Provincial pastor in RCCG, and they are blessed with three children.
 

Our Leadership Team

Pastor Andrew Oriunuebho

Pastor in charge of Admin and Finance

Pastor Joe Ochei

Pastor in charge of Church and Government

Pastor Adefolabi Adetunji

Pastor in charge of Teens and Youths

Pastor Olanrewaju Amusan

Pastor in charge
of CSR

FAQs

What is the difference between a Pentecostal church and the RCCG?

Jesus House Aberdeen is a parish of the RCCG, a global Pentecostal denomination founded in Nigeria in 1952. The RCCG operates under a unified doctrinal and leadership structure, whereas independent Pentecostal churches operate autonomously.

What are the credentials of the pastoral team at JHA?

Pastors hold RCCG ministerial ordination and have completed theological education. Supporting leaders are trained through internal RCCG programmes.
The full team adheres to RCCG global spiritual and administrative guidelines.

What are the key differences between worship at JHA and other churches?

JHA blends classic biblical and Pentecostal doctrine with contemporary community life, modern technology, and diverse events for all age groups — combining theological depth with practical, active engagement.

Jesus House Aberdeen is a parish of the RCCG, a global Pentecostal denomination founded in Nigeria in 1952. The RCCG operates under a unified doctrinal and leadership structure, whereas independent Pentecostal churches operate autonomously.

Pastors hold RCCG ministerial ordination and have completed theological education. Supporting leaders are trained through internal RCCG programmes.
The full team adheres to RCCG global spiritual and administrative guidelines.

JHA blends classic biblical and Pentecostal doctrine with contemporary community life, modern technology, and diverse events for all age groups — combining theological depth with practical, active engagement.

View More

Find Your Place at Jesus House Aberdeen

JOIN US TODAY

Jesus House Aberdeen, also known as City of God Church Aberdeen, is an international evangelical church
and pentecostal church in Aberdeen UK.
We are part of the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG) worldwide, established in 1952 by Rev. Josiah O. Akindayomi. RCCG is a growing church with parishes in over 178 countries. Pa Josiah Akindayomi died in 1980 at the age of 71, handing leadership to Pastor Enoch Dr Mark Igiehon is the Senior Pastor of RCCG Jesus House Aberdeen (City of God), a pentecostal church in Aberdeen, Scotland, and part of the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG) worldwide. He is a minister of the gospel by training and vocation, an evangelist, and a missioner who has led missions to many cities and nations. Dr Mark is a church planter who has led the planting of tens of churches across multiple cities and nations. He is a coach to many across the globe, including pastors, and has written books on many subjects, including the WisdomStreet series on practical wisdom for everyday successful living. Beyond his pastoral calling, Dr Mark is a lawyer by training, a strategist, and an advisor to leading corporations and governments. He brings extensive global experience from his career with Shell International. He is a Fellow at the University of Aberdeen Centre for Energy & Resources Law & Policy, where he teaches postgraduates and supervises doctoral candidates. He is also co-founder and chair of SONG (Success of Nations & Governments Organisation & Institute), focused on training and equipping people to contribute to successful and impactful government and public life. He is affiliated with the Singapore-based MDN (Making Disciples of Nations) network.\u2028<\/p>","text_color":"#000000","align":"justify","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Inter","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":16,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"custom","size":"180%","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet":{"unit":"custom","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile":{"unit":"custom","size":"","sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true},{"id":"fc734c1","elType":"container","settings":{"layout":"flexbox","flex_direction":"row","flex_justify_content":"space-between","flex_wrap":"nowrap","boxed_width":{"unit":"px","size":1600,"sizes":[]},"content_width":"full","width":{"unit":"%","size":100},"flex_gap":{"column":"0","row":"0","isLinked":true,"unit":"px","size":0},"border_border":"solid","border_width":{"unit":"px","top":"1","right":"1","bottom":"1","left":"1","isLinked":true},"border_color":"#0E672A","box_shadow_box_shadow_type":"yes","box_shadow_box_shadow":{"horizontal":0,"vertical":4,"blur":10,"spread":0,"color":"rgba(0, Pastor Alero Igiehon is an ordained minister and senior pastor in the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG) at Jesus House Aberdeen. She is also a trained Environmental and Business Improvement Specialist with over 20 years of experience in the Oil and Gas Industry. She has led and served in various capacities in the church, teaching and mentoring on biblical principles. She has been actively engaged in Marriage Preparation and Counselling ministry for over 20 years. Her desire is to see homes and christ centered marriages enjoy fruitfulness, peace, and joy. She is wife to Dr Mark Igiehon, a Provincial pastor in RCCG, and they are blessed with three children.

item.title: Junior Church
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
Junior Church

"Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit, and there are varieties of ministries, and the same Lord." — 1 Corinthians 12:4-5

WHO ARE WE?

Our Children’s Ministry is dedicated to nurturing young hearts and minds on a strong foundation rooted in God’s Word – The Bible. We aim to guide every child in building a meaningful relationship with God while supporting their growth into confident, compassionate individuals and future leaders who will positively impact the world for His glory.

OUR VISION

Through engaging Bible lessons delivered in a joyful and welcoming environment, we help children discover timeless truths and learn how to apply them in their everyday lives. Our approach makes learning about faith both meaningful and fun, encouraging curiosity, creativity, and spiritual growth.

We warmly welcome

children aged 2 to 12 years

every Sunday from 10:45am to 12:45pm

To ensure each child receives age-appropriate teaching and care, they are placed into one of four groups

Little Saints

2–3 years

Rising Stars

4–5 years

Risen Stars

6–8 years

Heritage

9–12 years

Each year, during our Children’s Day celebration on the last Sunday of May, we celebrate an important milestone as children who turn 12 by the end of May graduate into the Teens Church – marking an exciting new chapter in their spiritual journey.

We look forward to welcoming your child into a vibrant, caring, and faith-filled community where they can grow, learn, and thrive.

FAQs

Are there any membership fees or contributions expected?

There are no membership fees. Tithing (10% of income) is taught as a biblical principle and personal spiritual discipline, not a requirement. Ministry programmes including children’s church are generally free to attend.

What community outreach programmes does JHA offer?

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

How does JHA compare to other RCCG branches in Scotland for youth programmes?

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

There are no membership fees. Tithing (10% of income) is taught as a biblical principle and personal spiritual discipline, not a requirement. Ministry programmes including children's church are generally free to attend.

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

JOIN THE JUNIOR CHURCH

item.title: Daughters of the King (D.O.K.)
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
DAUGHTERS OF THE KING (DOK)

"Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit, and there are varieties of ministries, and the same Lord." — 1 Corinthians 12:4-5

WHO ARE WE?

Our Children’s Ministry is dedicated to nurturing young hearts and minds on a strong foundation rooted in God’s Word – The Bible. We aim to guide every child in building a meaningful relationship with God while supporting their growth into confident, compassionate individuals and future leaders who will positively impact the world for His glory.

OUR VISION

Our vision aligns with the RCCG Good Women's Fellowship globally:

  • To foster holistic development so that every woman will fulfil God’s purpose for her life.
  • To encourage women to discover, develop and maximise their God-given abilities.
  • Foster unity amongst the women folk.
  • Demonstrate the love of God to various groups of people both at home and abroad.
  • Empower women to become vessels of honour in their ministries, marital relationships, parenting, and careers.

OUR JOURNEY

2005

The journey of DOTK began in 2005 with just three women, including female ministers andministers’ wives, led by then Deaconess Alero Igiehon.
Their mission was to minister to other female members in hospitality, support with newborns, and child-minding. From these humble beginnings, the group grew steadily, reaching over 70 members by 2013.

2005 - 2013

Between 2005 and 2013, the group was simply referred to as the Good Women’s Fellowship, and under the
leadership of Assistant Pastor Alero Igiehon, held discussions after Sunday services, fostering a close-knit community. As the group expanded, Pastor Alero appointed female leaders
to manage the Fellowship, formally instituting an Executive committee in September 2013.

2016

In October 2016, the group was renamed ‘Daughters of the King’, based on recommendations to ensure inclusivity of all women, whether single or married, young and old.

Today

The story continues...

OUR IMPACT

The impact of DOTK extends beyond the church into the wider Aberdeen community through various community programs:

Annual Retreat: Annual retreat in November, a getaway event open to members and non-members to bond, recharge, relax, and have fun.

Workshops and Seminars: DOTK organises workshops, seminars, and prayer meetings to address everyday issues related to health, wellbeing, careers, business, relationships, and spiritual growth.

New Member Integration: New members are welcomed and supported through the DOTK community with welfare support and integration activities to help them settle in and feel at home in our faith community.

Annual Community Projects: Every year, DOTK demonstrates commitment to community service through gardening and maintenance activities at Friarsfield Park in Cults.

Church Project Support: DOTK actively supports church-wide projects including sponsoring painting, deep cleaning, and other impactful activities.

Voluntary Donations: On Mother's Day each year, DOTK contributes to the needs of the church by donating items for the junior church, equipment, and practical resources.

Wellbeing and Fitness Classes: Free exercise and fitness classes are arranged to encourage women to grow in physical health alongside their spiritual walk.

COMMUNITY PROJECTS

DOTK has successfully executed numerous community projects over the years

Painting Planters in Stewart Park, Hilton Area

Annual retreat in November, Getaway event

Gardening / maintenance at Friarsfield Park, Cults

LADIES GET AWAY TESTIMONIALS

“I've never attended any event like this. From the welcome and arrival to the dinner, all the activities, the food, the mirrors with our names, the hampers. Everything had a touch of excellence and I cannot wait for next year. You ladies created an atmosphere for rest and rejuvenation. God bless us all.”

Oge

“A big thank you to all our beautiful hosts and organisers. You are doing a great job putting smiles on people's faces with your yearly commitment to this great event. We appreciate your love and commitment towards your fellow sisters. The Good Lord will reward your labour of love. Remain blessed.”

Bimbo O

“I was thrilled, elated and de-stressed. I enjoyed the Ladies Get-Away. God bless all the ladies and the organisers. God will surely bless you all immensely.”

Chiamaka

FAQs

Are there any membership fees or contributions expected?

There are no membership fees. Tithing (10% of income) is taught as a biblical principle and personal spiritual discipline, not a requirement. Ministry programmes including children’s church are generally free to attend.

What community outreach programmes does JHA offer?

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

How does JHA compare to other RCCG branches in Scotland for youth programmes?

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

There are no membership fees. Tithing (10% of income) is taught as a biblical principle and personal spiritual discipline, not a requirement. Ministry programmes including children's church are generally free to attend.

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

    The impact of DOTK extends beyond the church into the wider Aberdeen community through various community programs:<\/p>","text_color":"#111827","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Inter","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":20,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.5,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"822c8ff","elType":"widget","settings":{"html":"

    Annual Retreat:<\/strong> Annual retreat in November, a getaway event open to members and non-members to bond, recharge, relax, and have fun.<\/p>

    Workshops and Seminars:<\/strong> DOTK organises workshops, seminars, and prayer meetings to address everyday issues related to health, wellbeing, careers, business, relationships, and spiritual growth.<\/p>

    New Member Integration:<\/strong> New members are welcomed and supported through the DOTK community with welfare support and integration activities to help them settle in and feel at home in our faith community.<\/p>

    Annual Community Projects:<\/strong> Every year, DOTK demonstrates commitment to community service through gardening and maintenance activities at Friarsfield Park in Cults.<\/p>

    Church Project Support:<\/strong> DOTK actively supports church-wide projects including sponsoring painting, deep cleaning, and other impactful activities.<\/p>

    Voluntary Donations:<\/strong> On Mother's Day each year, DOTK contributes to the needs of the church by donating items for the junior church, equipment, and practical resources.<\/p>

    Wellbeing and Fitness Classes:<\/strong> Free exercise and fitness classes are arranged to encourage women to grow in physical health alongside their spiritual walk.<\/p><\/div>"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"html"},{"id":"ae3d4ce","elType":"widget","settings":{"carousel_name":"Image     \u201cI've never attended any event like this. From the welcome and arrival to the dinner, all the activities, the food, the mirrors with our names, the hampers. Everything had a touch of excellence and I cannot wait for next year. You ladies created an atmosphere for rest and rejuvenation. God bless us all.\u201d<\/p>

    Oge<\/p><\/div>

    \u201cA big thank you to all our beautiful hosts and organisers. You are doing a great job putting smiles on people's faces with your yearly commitment to this great event.     Chiamaka<\/p><\/div><\/div>

{"timeout":1779920799,"value":{"content":"

DAUGHTERS OF THE KING (DOK)

"Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit, and there are varieties of ministries, and the same Lord." — 1 Corinthians 12:4-5

WHO ARE WE?

[elementor-element k="40ed5eee24cb0a9f9790410e2b603872" data=""]

OUR VISION

[elementor-element k="40ed5eee24cb0a9f9790410e2b603872" data=""]

OUR JOURNEY

2005

[elementor-element k="40ed5eee24cb0a9f9790410e2b603872" data=""]

2005 - 2013

[elementor-element k="40ed5eee24cb0a9f9790410e2b603872" data=""]

2016

[elementor-element k="40ed5eee24cb0a9f9790410e2b603872" data=""]

Today

[elementor-element k="40ed5eee24cb0a9f9790410e2b603872" data=""]

OUR IMPACT

[elementor-element k="40ed5eee24cb0a9f9790410e2b603872" data=""]

Annual Retreat: Annual retreat in November, a getaway event open to members and non-members to bond, recharge, relax, and have fun.

Workshops and Seminars: DOTK organises workshops, seminars, and prayer meetings to address everyday issues related to health, wellbeing, careers, business, relationships, and spiritual growth.

New Member Integration: New members are welcomed and supported through the DOTK community with welfare support and integration activities to help them settle in and feel at home in our faith community.

Annual Community Projects: Every year, DOTK demonstrates commitment to community service through gardening and maintenance activities at Friarsfield Park in Cults.

Church Project Support: DOTK actively supports church-wide projects including sponsoring painting, deep cleaning, and other impactful activities.

Voluntary Donations: On Mother's Day each year, DOTK contributes to the needs of the church by donating items for the junior church, equipment, and practical resources.

Wellbeing and Fitness Classes: Free exercise and fitness classes are arranged to encourage women to grow in physical health alongside their spiritual walk.

COMMUNITY PROJECTS

[elementor-element k="40ed5eee24cb0a9f9790410e2b603872" data=""]

Painting Planters in Stewart Park, Hilton Area

Annual retreat in November, Getaway event

Gardening / maintenance at Friarsfield Park, Cults

LADIES GET AWAY TESTIMONIALS

“I've never attended any event like this. From the welcome and arrival to the dinner, all the activities, the food, the mirrors with our names, the hampers. Everything had a touch of excellence and I cannot wait for next year. You ladies created an atmosphere for rest and rejuvenation. God bless us all.”

Oge

“A big thank you to all our beautiful hosts and organisers. You are doing a great job putting smiles on people's faces with your yearly commitment to this great event. We appreciate your love and commitment towards your fellow sisters. The Good Lord will reward your labour of love. Remain blessed.”

Bimbo O

“I was thrilled, elated and de-stressed. I enjoyed the Ladies Get-Away. God bless all the ladies and the organisers. God will surely bless you all immensely.”

Chiamaka

FAQs

[elementor-element k="40ed5eee24cb0a9f9790410e2b603872" data=""]
View More

JOIN THE DAUGHTERS OF THE KING

Join Us
","scripts":[],"styles":[]}}

item.title: Hello world!
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded: Welcome to WordPress. This is your first post. Edit or delete it, then start writing!

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: Hi, this is a comment. To get started with moderating, editing, and deleting comments, please visit the Comments screen in the dashboard. Commenter avatars come from Gravatar.

item.title: Sample Page
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
This is an example page. It's different from a blog post because it will stay in one place and will show up in your site navigation (in most themes). Most people start with an About page that introduces them to potential site visitors. It might say something like this:

Hi there! I'm a bike messenger by day, aspiring actor by night, and this is my website. I live in Los Angeles, have a great dog named Jack, and I like piña coladas. (And gettin' caught in the rain.)

...or something like this:

The XYZ Doohickey Company was founded in 1971, and has been providing quality doohickeys to the public ever since. Located in Gotham City, XYZ employs over 2,000 people and does all kinds of awesome things for the Gotham community.

As a new WordPress user, you should go to your dashboard to delete this page and create new pages for your content. Have fun!

item.title: Men Fellowship
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
KINGS MEN

"Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit, and there are varieties of ministries, and the same Lord." — 1 Corinthians 12:4-5

Our Goals

Support the mission of the RCCG by praying for leaders and actively participating in building and expanding the church.
  • Encourage one another to lead exemplary lives, living the word and teachings of Jesus Christ.

  • Take the lead in building Christ-centred marriages and families.

  • Build good relationships following the commandments of Christ.

  • Lead spirit-filled lives and be men of integrity.

  • Witness the Christian life and tell others.

FAQs

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

Are there any membership fees or contributions expected?

There are no membership fees. Tithing (10% of income) is taught as a biblical principle and personal spiritual discipline, not a requirement. Ministry programmes including children’s church are generally free to attend.

What are the key differences between worship at JHA and other churches?

JHA blends classic biblical and Pentecostal doctrine with contemporary community life, modern technology, and diverse events for all age groups — combining theological depth with practical, active engagement.

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

There are no membership fees. Tithing (10% of income) is taught as a biblical principle and personal spiritual discipline, not a requirement. Ministry programmes including children's church are generally free to attend.

JHA blends classic biblical and Pentecostal doctrine with contemporary community life, modern technology, and diverse events for all age groups — combining theological depth with practical, active engagement.

View More

JOIN THE MEN FELLOWSHIP

Join Us

item.title: Youth Central (The Hub)
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
Youth Central

"Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit, and there are varieties of ministries, and the same Lord." — 1 Corinthians 12:4-5

STRUCTURE

WHO ARE WE?

Youth Central is the youth ministry of Jesus House Aberdeen, committed to community growth, social enterprise, and projects that enhance the quality of life for young people across Aberdeen.
We empower young adults and teenagers to develop skills and become agents of positive change within their communities. Through recreational programs including sports, arts, and music, we promote self-expression, stress relief, and belonging.
We also offer counselling, life coaching, mentoring, educational support, and skills development to equip young people with the tools they need to succeed.

OUR VISION

To raise a generation of young people who are burning for God.

YouthCentral operates through four branches: Ablaze.819, Vizion, Tribe, and Sound of Zion.

ABLAZE 819

Ablaze.819 is our youth church for teenagers, running bimonthly on Sundays at 11:00 AM simultaneously with the main celebration service.
The fellowship is based on the Acts 2:41-47 model, centered on prayer and worship, bible teaching, baptism, shared participation, and breaking of bread.

Training & Conditioning

Professional coaching and fitness training programs

Competitive Teams

Join our basketball, soccer, and volleyball teams

Character Development

Build leadership, teamwork, and integrity

Weekly Practices

Tuesday & Thursday evenings, 6:30-8:00 PM

TRIBE – YOUNG ADULTS FELLOWSHIP

Tribe is a Jesus House initiative designed for young professionals and mature students aged 23 to 32.
At Tribe, we are committed to growing together into well-rounded individuals, spiritually, professionally, emotionally, and physically.
Tribe organises three core events each month:

Small Groups

Connect in intimate settings where authentic relationships are formed and everyone has a voice.

Weekly Meetings

Every Wednesday at 7 PM for engaging discussions, activities, and fellowship.

Multiple Locations

Meet at various homes and venues throughout the community for convenience.

SOUNDS OF ZION (SOZ) – YOUTH WORSHIP COLLECTIVE

Sounds of Zion is a youth collective that began in 2024, founded with the guidance of Pastor Mark and the Jesus House family. SOZ engages the youth within the church and provides a space for them to express themselves through authentic worship and music. Spreading the gospel through evangelism is at the heart of what SOZ does.
SOZ meets twice a year, once in summer and once in winter, and serves as a tool to bring both young and old back to the church across Aberdeen and Scotland.

Worship

Every Sunday

Rehearsals

Friday Nights

All Levels

Welcome

Get In Touch

VIZION – CHRISTIAN FELLOWSHIP AT ROBERT GORDON UNIVERSITY

Vizion Society is a christian fellowship within Robert Gordon University, providing a welcoming environment where members engage in bible study, prayer, fellowship, and discussions on personal development and life goals.
Vizion is open to Christians and non-Christians alike, aged 16 and above.

Inspiring Messages

Powerful talks that challenge you to dream bigger and live with purpose.

Personal Vision

Discover your unique calling and the impact you can make in the world.

Action Plans

Leave with practical steps to turn your vision into reality.

MORE YOUTH PICTURES

FAQs

How does JHA compare to other RCCG branches in Scotland for youth programmes?

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

What are the pros and cons of a large Pentecostal church versus a smaller fellowship?

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

View More

READY TO GET INVOLVED?

Don't miss out on these amazing opportunities to grow, serve, and have fun. Check our calendar for upcoming dates and registration details.

Join Us

item.title: Kingdom Theatre (K.T.)
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
KINGDOM THEATRE

Where Faith Meets the Stage

Support Us

THE WORD MADE FLESH

WHO ARE WE?

Since its inception in 2013, Kingdom Theatre has been dedicated to a single, powerful vision: transforming the timeless truths of Scripture into immersive, soul-stirring experiences. Through the dynamic fusion of drama and music, we bring the Word of God to life, making the stories of the Bible resonate with modern audiences in profound and creative ways.

Our MISSION

We believe that storytelling is one of the most effective ways to share the Gospel. Our goal is to illustrate the light of God through high-quality productions that inspire, challenge, and restore. From ancient biblical accounts to contemporary narratives of faith, Kingdom Theatre consistently delivers unique stories of:

  • Restoration: Showing how God elevates His people from low places to positions of destiny.
  • Hope: Reminding our community that even in times of exile or uncertainty, new "wells of mercy" are waiting to be found.
  • Redemption: Bringing the journey of Christ and His followers to centre stage with fresh perspective and creative depth.

CREATIVE EXCELLENCE

Kingdom Theatre is more than a drama group - it is a hub for creative collaboration.

Our productions are original works - written, directed, and produced by our talented in-house team.

By blending musical theatre with powerful dramatic performances, we create a space where art and
evangelism meet.

Whether it is a major conference production or a seasonal musical, every show is crafted to point the audience back to the ultimate Creator.

Support Us

TAKING THE LIGHT TO THE WORLD

As we grow, our vision extends beyond the walls of the church.
Kingdom Theatre is committed to taking our productions into the wider world, using the stage as a platform to share the light of God with the city of Aberdeen and beyond. We invite you to join us as we continue to tell the stories that matter most.

FAQs

What are the key differences between worship at JHA and other churches?

JHA blends classic biblical and Pentecostal doctrine with contemporary community life, modern technology, and diverse events for all age groups — combining theological depth with practical, active engagement.

What community outreach programmes does JHA offer?

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

Which Aberdeen church offers the best blend of traditional teaching and modern activities?

JHA youth programmes align with RCCG global frameworks covering Bible study, mentorship, talent development, and leadership training. Local differences between RCCG branches reflect resources and community demographics, not theological approach.

JHA blends classic biblical and Pentecostal doctrine with contemporary community life, modern technology, and diverse events for all age groups — combining theological depth with practical, active engagement.

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

JHA youth programmes align with RCCG global frameworks covering Bible study, mentorship, talent development, and leadership training. Local differences between RCCG branches reflect resources and community demographics, not theological approach.

View More

ARE YOU A WRITER, ACTOR, MUSICIAN, OR TECHNICIAN WITH A PASSION FOR THE ARTS AND THE KINGDOM?

We are always looking for dedicated individuals to join our creative family.

Get Involved

item.title: The Choir
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
THE CHOIR

"Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit, and there are varieties of ministries, and the same Lord." — 1 Corinthians 12:4-5

WHO ARE WE?

The Jesus House Aberdeen choir has been taking their music ministry beyond the four walls of the church to the streets, sharing the love of God and spreading joy through a series of outreach events across the city.

VOICES OF HOPE

From singing at local shopping centres and community events to performing at care homes, the choir has been using its talents to bring hope and cheer to those around them. Their energetic and uplifting ministrations have been a hit with audiences of all ages and have helped to share the message of God's love and redemption with the community.

One of the highlights of the choir's outreach efforts was a recent ministration at the West Hill Care Home, where residents and staff were treated to a special concert featuring a range of Christmas carols and hymns. The event was a huge success, with many residents and their families with staff members commenting on the positive impact the carol had on their day and having to bring Christmas to the home.

Other outreaches are:

Gospel at the mall during our 20th Anniversary
Ministration at Balmoral Castle at the invitation of the Royal Family
Ministration at Society of Petroleum Engineers Anniversary
Ministration at 30th Anniversary of African Women
Christmas at the Mall

The Jesus House Aberdeen choir's outreach efforts are an important part of the church's mission to serve the community and share the love of God through music. By taking their music out into the community, the choir is able to reach people who may not be able to attend regular church services and bring a message of hope and joy to those who need it most.

FAQs

What community outreach programmes does JHA offer?

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

What are the key differences between worship at JHA and other churches?

JHA blends classic biblical and Pentecostal doctrine with contemporary community life, modern technology, and diverse events for all age groups — combining theological depth with practical, active engagement.

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

JHA blends classic biblical and Pentecostal doctrine with contemporary community life, modern technology, and diverse events for all age groups — combining theological depth with practical, active engagement.

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

View More

JOIN THE CHOIR

Join Us

item.title: Community Impact
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
COMMUNITY IMPACT

From daily essentials to long-term empowerment, our Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives are designed to uplift every generation.

Join Us

OUR OUTREACH PILLARS

Compassion & Essential Support
  • The JHA Food Bank: A market-style experience where you can bring your own bags to pick out essentials.
  • Everyday Food Basket: A 24/7 grab-and-go resource at our doorstep for those in immediate need.
  • Medical Outreach: Periodic health checks and clinics focused on both physical and emotional wellbeing.
  • Prayer & Chat Line: A dedicated space for a listening ear, spiritual guidance, and prayer support.
  • Tea, Coffee, & Chitchat: Monthly informal conversations right at the church doorstep to foster community connection.
Growth, Skills & Empowerment
  • Job Club: Professional coaching in CV writing, interview preparation, and employability skills.
  • Entrepreneurial Club: Empowering small businesses and local startups to thrive.
  • DIY Club & Projects: Hands-on workshops for creativity and practical skill-building.
  • Parchment Bookstore: Providing life-changing Christian resources for all ages.
Next Gen & Youth Development
  • Teens Support Study Club: Academic mentorship and study support for ages 12–18.
  • Kiddies Club: Engaging talent shows, movie nights, and family-focused excursions.
  • New Army: A vibrant movement focused on youth empowerment and spiritual growth.
  • SBIS (Summer Bible Institute of Scotland): Free, accessible Bible training for all ages.
Fellowship, Family & Wellbeing
  • Football & Fitness: Combining amateur sports and fellowship with a mission of evangelism.
  • Blissful Mates: Free professional counseling, marriage preparation, and conflict resolution.
  • Senior Ladies Knitting Club: Community-focused charity knitting led by Elder Joanne.
  • Summertime BBQ: Our annual season of family fun and neighborhood bonding.
Seasonal & Creative Outreach
  • Choir Outreach: Bringing the joy of the Gospel through seasonal carols at malls, local parks, and Aberdeen FC.
  • Christmas Smile Tree Project: Spreading festive hope through targeted gift-giving and community outreach.

JOIN THE MOVEMENT

As we continue to touch lives through our daily, weekly, and annual events, we invite you to be the hands and feet of Jesus. Whether you volunteer your time, share your story, or show up to support our neighbors, your involvement makes a difference.

Let’s continue to be a church that not only prays but acts, not only gathers but gives, and not only worships but works.

Together, we are Touching Lives, Transforming Communities.

Join Us

FAQs

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

What community outreach programmes does JHA offer in Aberdeen?

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

What are the pros and cons of a large Pentecostal church versus a smaller fellowship?

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

SonRise Service

Begin your Sunday in the presence of God with our early morning service.

SUNDAY

Sunday School

10:30 AM

A time of in-depth Bible study for all ages before the main service.

SUNDAY

Celebration Service

11:00 AM

Our flagship Sunday service - full of praise, powerful preaching and family fellowship.

TUESDAY

Bible Exposition

6:30 PM - 8:00 PM

Midweek deep dive into God’s World. Come hungry, leave transformed.

THURSDAY

Prayer Meeting

6:00 PM - 7:45 PM

United in intercession - praying for our church, city and the nations.

BEYOND WEEKLY SERVICES,
WE HOST MANY SPECIALISED PROGRAMS.

FAQs

What is the difference between a Pentecostal church and the RCCG?

Jesus House Aberdeen is a parish of the RCCG, a global Pentecostal denomination founded in Nigeria in 1952. The RCCG operates under a unified doctrinal and leadership structure, whereas independent Pentecostal churches operate autonomously.

How does a baptism service at JHA typically work for adults?

Candidates attend baptismal classes, share their testimony of faith, and are baptised by full immersion during a Sunday service or dedicated event. Family and friends are encouraged to attend and celebrate.

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

Jesus House Aberdeen is a parish of the RCCG, a global Pentecostal denomination founded in Nigeria in 1952. The RCCG operates under a unified doctrinal and leadership structure, whereas independent Pentecostal churches operate autonomously.

Candidates attend baptismal classes, share their testimony of faith, and are baptised by full immersion during a Sunday service or dedicated event. Family and friends are encouraged to attend and celebrate.

item.title: Giving Page
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
GIVING PAGE

And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men; knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.” — Colossians 3:23-24

PARTNER WITH OUR MISSION

The mission of Jesus House Aberdeen is to spread the gospel across the nations. We rely on charitable donations to accomplish this mission. Your giving funds church planters, evangelism, community outreach, youth programmes and much more.

The Digital Giving Envelope

Your giving is secure and processed through our verified payment portal.

Ready to give? Complete your donation securely via PayPal:

DONATE VIA PAYPAL

GIFT AID & TAXPAYER AID DECLARATION

I declare that I pay income tax and/or capital gains tax at the basic/higher rate of tax to cover the gift aid repayment on all the charities to which I donate and will notify the above charity when I cease to be a taxpayer.

I understand that:

  • I can cancel this declaration at any time by notifying Jesus House Aberdeen.
  • The charity will reclaim 25p of tax on every £1 that I give on or after 6 April 2008.
  • I MUST pay an amount of income tax and/or capital gains tax at least equal to the tax that Jesus House Aberdeen will claim on my donations in the tax year (currently 25p for every £1 I give).
  • I MUST notify Jesus House of any change in my name and address details.
*please note that your signature above gives us authorization as though you were physically present whilst your card is being charged

*please note that ticking the box is equivalent to your signature in this instance which gives us authorization as though you were physically present whilst your card is being charged

EVERY GIFT MAKES A DIFFERENCE

“And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men; Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ…” (Col 3:23-24)

PROJECT NEHEMIAH Y20

Arise & Build for the Lord

We are standing at a pivotal moment in the church's history. Project Nehemiah Y20 is our collective commitment to honoring God by securing our foundations and investing in the generations to come.

With a faith-filled goal of £1,000,000, we are rising together to build a legacy that outlives us.

LEARN MORE

HOW TO SUPPORT US?

Your contribution is a seed sown into the spiritual and physical growth of our community.
We invite you to give prayerfully as we work toward our £1,000,000 goal.

Support the Vision

Account Name: RCCG Jesus House Aberdeen
Bank: The Royal Bank of Scotland PLC
Sort Code: 83-15-31
Account Number: 19102034
Reference: Nehemiah Y20

Together, let us arise and build for the glory of the Lord.

FAQs

Are there any membership fees or contributions expected?

There are no membership fees. Tithing (10% of income) is taught as a biblical principle and personal spiritual discipline, not a requirement. Ministry programmes including children’s church are generally free to attend.

What is the average donation expected for tithing at JHA?

The biblical tithe is 10% of gross income, but no mandatory amount is imposed. Giving is a personal decision. Offerings above the tithe support specific projects and missions, and the church communicates transparently how funds are used.

Are there any independent audits of RCCG parishes' financial transparency in the UK?

As a registered UK charity, JHA files annual accounts with the Charity Commission. The RCCG UK central administration sets financial policies for parishes, providing a framework for accountability and oversight.

There are no membership fees. Tithing (10% of income) is taught as a biblical principle and personal spiritual discipline, not a requirement. Ministry programmes including children's church are generally free to attend.

The biblical tithe is 10% of gross income, but no mandatory amount is imposed. Giving is a personal decision. Offerings above the tithe support specific projects and missions, and the church communicates transparently how funds are used.

As a registered UK charity, JHA files annual accounts with the Charity Commission. The RCCG UK central administration sets financial policies for parishes, providing a framework for accountability and oversight.

item.title: What’s On
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
LIFE AT JHA: OUR ANNUAL CALENDAR

A SEASON FOR EVERYTHING

At Jesus House Aberdeen, our calendar is a reflection of our journey together in faith. Throughout the year, we gather for moments of deep spiritual renewal, vibrant cultural celebration, and community outreach. While our weekly services remain our heartbeat, these key annual events mark the milestones of our church family.

THEME OF THE YEAR

MAJOR SEASONAL EVENTS — THE BIG FOUR

THE NEW YEAR

Thanksgiving Sunday

WHEN: The first Sunday of January

We begin every year by giving God the glory for what is ahead. This is a vibrant, colorful celebration where we come together in our traditional and cultural attire to offer a sacrifice of praise for the new year.

SPRING

Thanksgiving Sunday

WHEN: March / April

From the solemn reflection of Good Friday to the joyful triumph of Easter Sunday, we celebrate the Resurrection with special services and community-wide events that honour the sacrifice of Christ.

SUMMER

Thanksgiving Sunday

WHEN: July

Our flagship summer conference is a season of intense spiritual encounter, powerful teaching, and divine transformation. It is a dedicated time for the JHA family to seek God’s face and receive a fresh word for the months ahead.

Read More

WINTER

Thanksgiving Sunday

WHEN: December

We take the joy of the Gospel to the heart of Aberdeen. Look out for our choir at the local malls and join us in-house for our annual Christmas Carol service, a night of music, light, and festive celebration.

OTHER ACTIVITIES

Beyond our corporate gatherings, our various ministries host annual retreats and social events designed to foster deeper connection, spiritual refreshment, and fun.

Daughters of the Great King Annual Retreat

Women's Ministry

DOK Page

Youth Annual Retreat

Ages 14 - 18+

Youth Page

Children’s Summer Camp

Our Youngest Members

Children’s Page

Departmental Summer Barbecue / End of Year Gatherings

Fellowship & Community

Join a team Page

Beyond our annual milestones, we have a variety of monthly and quarterly events including SBIS training, Entrepreneurial Club workshops, and community outreach days.
Follow us on our social media platforms to get updated on events/activities weekly.

FAQs

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

What community outreach programmes does JHA offer in Aberdeen?

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

What are the pros and cons of a large Pentecostal church versus a smaller fellowship?

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

item.title: Our Training Centre
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
OUR TRAINING CENTRE

Join Us

ABOUT OUR TRAININGS

At Jesus House Aberdeen, we are a Teaching Church. Part of our mission is to equip people with the wisdom and skills needed to lead impactful lives. Whether you are a long time member or a global professional seeking growth, our doors are open to you.Explore our curated pathways; ranging from foundational RCCG Certifications to Bespoke Leadership Trainings designed for everyone, regardless of church affiliation.

THE TRAINING STAGES

Discovery (or Rediscovery) Class

Discovering your identity in Christ and finding your specific purpose and God given gifts.

Baptismal Class

For new believers or those looking to publicly declare their faith through water baptism by immersion.
Covers the tenets of the faith and the ‘New Birth’ experience.

Workers in Training (WIT)

8-week course equipping members for active service (choir, ushers, scripture teachers).

School of Discipleship (SOD)

10-month course (one Saturday monthly) producing well-grounded disciples who understand spiritual warfare.

Bible College

Advanced theological education.

Discovery (or Rediscovery) Class

Discovering your identity in Christ and finding your specific purpose and God given gifts.

Baptismal Class

For new believers or those looking to publicly declare their faith through water baptism by immersion.
Covers the tenets of the faith and the 'New Birth' experience.

Workers in Training (WIT)

8-week course equipping members for active service (choir, ushers, scripture teachers).

School of Discipleship (SOD)

10-month course (one Saturday monthly) producing well-grounded disciples who understand spiritual warfare.

Bible College

Advanced theological education.

INTERESTED IN ANY OF THESE TRAININGS?

Fill this form

VISIT US DURING SERVICE HOURS

Alternatively, you are welcome to visit us during regular church hours. Our doors and hearts are always open.

SUNDAY

9:00 AM

First Service

11:00 AM

Second Service

TUESDAY

7:00 PM

Midweek Service

LOCATION

Jesus House @ City of God, 20 Holburn,

Aberdeen, AB10 6BT

Learn More

FAQs

How does a baptism service at JHA typically work for adults?

Candidates attend baptismal classes, share their testimony of faith, and are baptised by full immersion during a Sunday service or dedicated event. Family and friends are encouraged to attend and celebrate.

Can you explain the significance of water baptism in the RCCG context?

Water baptism at JHA is by full immersion, symbolising death to sin and resurrection in Christ. It is an outward declaration of inward faith, required after accepting Jesus as Lord and Saviour — not a means of salvation itself.

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

Candidates attend baptismal classes, share their testimony of faith, and are baptised by full immersion during a Sunday service or dedicated event. Family and friends are encouraged to attend and celebrate.

Water baptism at JHA is by full immersion, symbolising death to sin and resurrection in Christ. It is an outward declaration of inward faith, required after accepting Jesus as Lord and Saviour — not a means of salvation itself.

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

item.title: Join-A-Team
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
JOIN A TEAM

Join Us

JOIN THE MISSION AT JHA

At Jesus House Aberdeen, we believe that everyone has a unique gift to offer. We are blessed with a vibrant community of people dedicated to serving our church, our city, and the Kingdom of God. Whether it's behind the scenes or on the front lines, there is a place for you to make a difference.

READY TO GET INVOLVED?

Click the button below to explore our ministries and sign up. For a deeper dive into how our various teams operate, you can download our Volunteer Information Guide.

Explore Our Ministries

JOIN A TEAM

FAQs

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

What community outreach programmes does JHA offer?

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

What are the pros and cons of a large Pentecostal church versus a smaller fellowship?

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

A large church like JHA offers diverse age-specific ministries, professional production, a large peer community, and broad community impact. Smaller fellowships offer tighter community feel. The best choice depends on personal preference for size and ministry needs.

View More

item.title: Counselling, Prayer & Holistic Wellbeing
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
Counselling

Join Us

YOU DO NOT HAVE TO WALK ALONE

At Jesus House Aberdeen, we believe healing and wholeness come through faith, community, and compassionate care. Our Counselling, Prayer and Holistic Wellbeing ministry offers a safe, confidential space for every individual to find support, restoration, and hope.

Whether you are navigating grief, anxiety, relationship difficulties, or simply seeking guidance, our trained counsellors and prayer leads are here to walk alongside you.

OUR COMMITMENT TO COMPREHENSIVE CARE

We Are Here for You. Body. Mind. Spirit.

BodyMindSpirit

Whether you are facing a significant crisis, navigating a difficult season, or simply feel the need for support, we are dedicated to providing the highest standard of compassionate care. Our counselling services are designed to address the specific and unique needs of each person. We recognise that true healing is holistic, which is why our teams are structured to offer expertise across a wide range of specialised areas. Beyond spiritual guidance, we offer practical support for your mental, emotional, and physical well-being. Everyone is welcome, both our members and the wider community, and all interactions are held in the strictest confidence.

MEET OUR SPECIALISED COUNSELLING & PRAYER LEADS

Pastor Alero Igiehon

Head of Marriage & Family Counselling (Blissful Mates)

Pastor Alero leads our Blissful Mates initiative, providing free, compassionate, and expert counselling for couples navigating relationship challenges, preparing for marriage, or seeking conflict resolution. Her heart is to see relationships restored and families strengthened in faith and love.

Dr Habiba Yamusa

Head of Health and Wellness Counselling

Dr Habiba Yamusa alongside other practitioners provides a supportive, confidential space for those navigating health and wellness challenges, emotional distress, or mental health concerns. Focusing on the intricate connection between physical and emotional well-being, his goal is to help you find peace and resilience in God.

Deaconess Rita Gibson

Head of Prayer Counselling

Deaconess Rita leads our vibrant Prayer Ministry. If you need spiritual strength, prayer counselling, or simply want someone to stand with you in faith, her team is available. They offer a compassionate space for spiritual intercession and comfort.

Mrs Awele Omaruaye

Head of Physical Health and Wellbeing

Mrs Awele champions physical health within our faith community. Whether you are seeking to improve your fitness, manage weight challenges, or adopt a healthier lifestyle, she provides practical, faith-inspired guidance to help you honour your body.

CONNECT WITH A COUNSELLOR

We encourage you to reach out, regardless of the challenge you face.
Every conversation is a confidential and safe space. If you are uncertain which team best meets your current need, simply indicate that in your message, and we will guide you to the right support.

VISIT US DURING SERVICE HOURS

Alternatively, you are welcome to visit us during regular church hours. Our doors and hearts are always open.

SUNDAY

9:00 AM

First Service

11:00 AM

Second Service

Learn More

FAQs

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

What community outreach programmes does JHA offer?

JHA runs a food bank, health awareness events, skills workshops, festive outreach, and volunteers with local charities addressing homelessness, poverty, and education across Aberdeen.

How reliable is the spiritual guidance offered at JHA for young adults?

JHA provides dedicated young adults’ fellowships, Bible studies, mentorship, and one-on-one pastoral access. Guidance covers faith, career, relationships, mental health, and leadership development within a Christian framework.

View More
