Learn More

FAQs

Is joining Jesus House Aberdeen worth it for someone new to faith?

JHA offers structured biblical teaching, pastoral care, fellowship, and mentorship within a welcoming community. It actively supports new members in integrating into church life and deepening their faith.

What is the difference between a Pentecostal church and the RCCG?

Jesus House Aberdeen is a parish of the RCCG, a global Pentecostal denomination founded in Nigeria in 1952. The RCCG operates under a unified doctrine and leadership structure, whereas independent Pentecostal churches operate autonomously.

Are there any membership fees or contributions expected?

There are no membership fees at JHA. We believe in giving freely and joyfully. Any financial contributions are entirely voluntary and made as an act of worship, not obligation.

Jesus House Aberdeen is a parish of the RCCG, a global Pentecostal denomination founded in Nigeria in 1952. The RCCG operates under a unified doctrinal and leadership structure, whereas independent Pentecostal churches operate autonomously.

item.title: Cookie Policy
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
Cookie Policy (UK Compliant)

Last updated: 24 May 2026

This Cookie Policy explains how Jesus House of Aberdeen ("we", "us", "our") uses cookies and similar technologies on https://jesushouseaberdeen.org. This policy should be read alongside our Privacy Policy.

1. What Are Cookies?

Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They help websites function properly, improve user experience, and provide information about how the site is used. Cookies may be "persistent" (stored until deleted) or "session" cookies (deleted when you close your browser).

2. How We Use Cookies

We use cookies to:

  • Ensure the website functions correctly
  • Improve website performance
  • Understand how visitors use our site
  • Remember user preferences
  • Support marketing and advertising activity (where applicable)

We do not use cookies to collect sensitive personal information.

3. Types of Cookies We Use

a) Strictly Necessary Cookies

These cookies are essential for the operation of our website. They enable core functionality such as security, network management, and accessibility. These cookies cannot be disabled.

b) Performance and Analytics Cookies

These cookies help us understand how visitors interact with our website by collecting anonymous information. They allow us to analyse:

  • Page visits
  • Traffic sources
  • User behaviour
  • Site performance

We use this information to improve our website. Examples may include Google Analytics and similar analytics tools. These cookies are only set with your consent.

c) Functionality Cookies

These cookies remember choices you make, such as:

  • Language preferences
  • Region
  • Previously submitted information

They improve your experience but are not essential. These cookies are only set with your consent.

d) Marketing and Advertising Cookies

These cookies are used to:

  • Track visitors across websites
  • Display relevant advertisements
  • Measure marketing effectiveness

They may be set by us or by third-party providers. These cookies are only used where you have given consent.

4. Third-Party Cookies

Some cookies are placed by third-party services that appear on our website. These may include:

  • Analytics providers
  • Embedded content providers
  • Marketing platforms

We do not control these cookies. Please refer to the relevant third-party privacy policies for further information.

5. Managing Your Cookie Preferences

When you first visit our website, you will be presented with a cookie banner allowing you to accept or manage cookies. You can change your preferences at any time by:

  • Using our cookie management tool (if available), or
  • Adjusting your browser settings

Most browsers allow you to:

  • View cookies
  • Delete cookies
  • Block cookies
  • Receive warnings before cookies are stored

Please note that disabling cookies may affect website functionality.

6. Legal Basis for Using Cookies

Under UK law (PECR and UK GDPR):

  • Strictly necessary cookies are used on the basis of legitimate interests
  • All other cookies are used only with your consent
  • You may withdraw consent at any time

7. Data Collected Through Cookies

Information collected via cookies may include:

  • IP address (anonymised where possible)
  • Browser type
  • Device information
  • Pages visited
  • Time spent on site
  • Referring URLs

This data is used in accordance with our Privacy Policy.

8. Updates to This Policy

We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with a revised date.

9. Contact Us

If you have questions about our use of cookies, please contact:

Email: admin@jesushouseaberdeen.org
Phone: +447769991731
Address: 20 Holburn Street, Aberdeen, AB10 6BT

Last updated: 24 May 2026<\/strong><\/p>

This Cookie Policy explains how Jesus House of Aberdeen (\"we\", \"us\", \"our\") uses cookies and similar technologies on https:\/\/jesushouseaberdeen.org. This policy should be read alongside our Privacy Policy.<\/p>

1. What Are Cookies?<\/h2>

Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They help websites function properly, improve user experience, and provide information about how the site is used. Cookies may be \"persistent\" (stored until deleted) or \"session\" cookies (deleted when you close your browser).<\/p>

2. How We Use Cookies<\/h2>

We use cookies to:<\/p>

  • Ensure the website functions correctly<\/li>
  • Improve website performance<\/li>
  • Understand how visitors use our site<\/li>
  • Remember user preferences<\/li>
  • Support marketing and advertising activity (where applicable)<\/li><\/ul>

    We do not use cookies to collect sensitive personal information.<\/p>

    3. Types of Cookies We Use<\/h2>

    a) Strictly Necessary Cookies<\/h3>

    These cookies are essential for the operation of our website. They enable core functionality such as security, network management, and accessibility. These cookies cannot be disabled.<\/p>

    b) Performance and Analytics Cookies<\/h3>

    These cookies help us understand how visitors interact with our website by collecting anonymous information. They allow us to analyse:<\/p>

    • Page visits<\/li>
    • Traffic sources<\/li>
    • User behaviour<\/li>
    • Site performance<\/li><\/ul>

      We use this information to improve our website. Examples may include Google Analytics and similar analytics tools. These cookies are only set with your consent.<\/p>

      c) Functionality Cookies<\/h3>

      These cookies remember choices you make, such as:<\/p>

      • Language preferences<\/li>
      • Region<\/li>
      • Previously submitted information<\/li><\/ul>

        They improve your experience but are not essential. These cookies are only set with your consent.<\/p>

        d) Marketing and Advertising Cookies<\/h3>

        These cookies are used to:<\/p>

        • Track visitors across websites<\/li>
        • Display relevant advertisements<\/li>
        • Measure marketing effectiveness<\/li><\/ul>

          They may be set by us or by third-party providers. These cookies are only used where you have given consent.<\/p>

          4. Third-Party Cookies<\/h2>

          Some cookies are placed by third-party services that appear on our website. These may include:<\/p>

          • Analytics providers<\/li>
          • Embedded content providers<\/li>
          • Marketing platforms<\/li><\/ul>

            We do not control these cookies. Please refer to the relevant third-party privacy policies for further information.<\/p>

            5. Managing Your Cookie Preferences<\/h2>

            When you first visit our website, you will be presented with a cookie banner allowing you to accept or manage cookies. You can change your preferences at any time by:<\/p>

            • Using our cookie management tool (if available), or<\/li>
            • Adjusting your browser settings<\/li><\/ul>

              Most browsers allow you to:<\/p>

              • View cookies<\/li>
              • Delete cookies<\/li>
              • Block cookies<\/li>
              • Receive warnings before cookies are stored<\/li><\/ul>

                Please note that disabling cookies may affect website functionality.<\/p>

                6. Legal Basis for Using Cookies<\/h2>

                Under UK law (PECR and UK GDPR):<\/p>

                • Strictly necessary cookies are used on the basis of legitimate interests<\/li>
                • All other cookies are used only with your consent<\/li>
                • You may withdraw consent at any time<\/li><\/ul>

                  7. Data Collected Through Cookies<\/h2>

                  Information collected via cookies may include:<\/p>

                  • IP address (anonymised where possible)<\/li>
                  • Browser type<\/li>
                  • Device information<\/li>
                  • Pages visited<\/li>
                  • Time spent on site<\/li>
                  • Referring URLs<\/li><\/ul>

                    This data is used in accordance with our Privacy Policy.<\/p>

                    8. Updates to This Policy<\/h2>

                    We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with a revised date.<\/p>

                    9. Contact Us<\/h2>

                    If you have questions about our use of cookies, please contact:<\/p>

                    Email:<\/strong> admin@jesushouseaberdeen.org
                    Phone:<\/strong> +447769991731
                    Address:<\/strong> 20 Holburn Street, Aberdeen, AB10 6BT<\/p>","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Inter","typography_font_weight":"400"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":false}]

item.title: Terms of Use
item.dc:creator: Janne
item.content:encoded:
Website Terms of Use

Last updated: 24 May 2026

These Terms of Use govern your use of the website https://jesushouseaberdeen.org operated by Jesus House of Aberdeen ("we", "us", "our"). By accessing or using our website, you agree to these Terms of Use. If you do not agree, you should not use our website.

1. About Us

Organization Name: Jesus House of Aberdeen
Registered Office: 20 Holburn Street, Aberdeen, AB10 6BT
Email: admin@jesushouseaberdeen.org

We are registered in England and Wales.

2. Use of Our Website

You may use our website for lawful purposes only. You agree not to:

  • Use the website in any way that breaches applicable law
  • Interfere with the security or functionality of the site
  • Attempt to gain unauthorised access to any systems
  • Use the site to transmit harmful or malicious material
  • Copy, scrape, or reproduce content without permission

We reserve the right to restrict or terminate access where misuse occurs.

3. Website Content

All content on this website, including text, graphics, branding, logos, and design, is owned by or licensed to us and is protected by intellectual property laws.

You may:

  • View and print content for personal or internal business use

You may not:

  • Reproduce, distribute, or commercially exploit our content without written permission

4. Information Disclaimer

The content on this website is provided for general information purposes only. It does not constitute:

  • Professional advice
  • Legal advice
  • Financial advice
  • Commercial advice

You should not rely on website content as the sole basis for business decisions. We recommend seeking appropriate professional advice where necessary.

5. Services and Enquiries

Submitting an enquiry through our website does not create a contractual relationship. Any services we provide are governed by separate written agreements. We reserve the right to accept or decline enquiries at our discretion.

6. Accuracy of Information

We make reasonable efforts to ensure that information on our website is accurate and up to date. However, we do not guarantee that:

  • Content is complete
  • Content is error-free
  • Content is current

We may update or remove content at any time without notice.

7. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, we shall not be liable for:

  • Any loss or damage arising from use of our website
  • Loss of profits, revenue, data, or business opportunities
  • Indirect or consequential losses
  • Reliance on website content

Nothing in these Terms limits liability for:

  • Death or personal injury caused by negligence
  • Fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation
  • Any liability that cannot be excluded under law

8. Third-Party Links

Our website may contain links to third-party websites. These links are provided for convenience only. We do not control and are not responsible for the content, policies, or practices of third-party sites. Accessing third-party websites is at your own risk.

9. User-Submitted Content

Where users submit information via forms or communications:

  • You confirm that the information is accurate
  • You have the right to submit it
  • It does not infringe third-party rights

We reserve the right to remove or refuse content that is unlawful, offensive, or inappropriate.

10. Data Protection

We process personal data in accordance with our Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy. Please refer to those policies for further information.

11. Availability of Website

We aim to make our website available at all times. However, we do not guarantee uninterrupted access and may suspend, withdraw, or restrict the site for maintenance or operational reasons.

12. Changes to These Terms

We may revise these Terms of Use at any time. Updated terms will be published on this page and will take effect immediately. Continued use of the website constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.

13. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

These Terms of Use are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Any disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales.

14. Contact Us

If you have any questions about these Terms of Use, please contact:

Email: admin@jesushouseaberdeen.org
Phone: +447769991731
Address: 20 Holburn Street, Aberdeen, AB10 6BT
